rationale: Deployment tool registration is now limited to the privileged agent_runtime fleet instead of every worker dispatcher, keeping stack update locking and execution on the intended deployment-capable worker boundary.
Compose pull and up result mappings are now strictly checked for success signals and non-zero exit codes fail closed before later lifecycle steps.
Deployment execution now writes command-log and after-state evidence from a finally path when runner calls fail after before-state capture.
deployment.update_compose_stack registration moved inside the agent_runtime fleet setup block rather than using the default disabled runner on all workers.
Command-log evidence is persisted before verification on success and in the failure cleanup path when pull or up fails.
Top-level automated review summary; its actionable inline findings are tracked by their individual comment IDs.
The awaited first deployment task result is now assigned and asserted as COMPLETED.
_parse_inputs now rejects non-allowlisted stack names at the execution boundary with INVALID_INPUT.
removeOrphans and wait now require real boolean values and reject string payloads with INVALID_INPUT.
desiredStateRef was removed from emitted outputs so runtime output stays within the declared tool schema.
Top-level Codex review wrapper; actionable suggestions are tracked by their individual inline comment IDs.
The awaited first deployment task result is now assigned and asserted as COMPLETED.
